metric_name: Hours per year constant
notes: Base constant for converting MTBF in hours to years

metric_name: MaXLine individual drive MTBF
notes: MaXLine rated MTBF = 1 million hours

metric_name: FC drive individual drive MTBF
notes: FC drive rated MTBF = 1.2 million hours — 20% higher than MaXLine at individual drive level

metric_name: MaXLine capacity specification
metric_value: 250GB raw; 200GB at 80% loading; 5 drives per TB at 80% loading
notes: 80% loading assumption applied; 5 drives/TB is the key driver of MaXLine system MTBF advantage

metric_name: FC disk drive capacity specification
metric_value: 73GB raw; 58.4GB at 80% loading; 17.12 drives per TB at 80% loading
notes: 80% loading assumption applied; 17.12 drives/TB is why FC system MTBF is far lower per TB

metric_name: RAID multiplier configurations tested
notes: Higher RAID multiplier = more drives per TB = lower system MTBF

metric_name: Config 1: MaXLine 250GB RAID-2x system MTBF per TB
notes: MaXLine 250GB; RAID 2x; 10 RAID drives/TB; system MTBF = 11.41 years

metric_name: Config 1: FC 73GB RAID-2x system MTBF per TB
notes: FC 73GB; RAID 2x; 34.25 RAID drives/TB; system MTBF = 3.997 years — MaXLine wins by 2.85x

metric_name: Config 2: MaXLine 250GB RAID-5x system MTBF per TB
notes: MaXLine 250GB; RAID 5x; 25 RAID drives/TB; system MTBF = 4.563 years

metric_name: Config 2: FC 73GB RAID-5x system MTBF per TB
notes: FC 73GB; RAID 5x; 85.6 RAID drives/TB; system MTBF = 1.599 years — MaXLine wins by 2.85x

metric_name: Config 3: MaXLine 250GB RAID-3x system MTBF per TB
notes: MaXLine 250GB; RAID 3x; 15 RAID drives/TB; system MTBF = 7.605 years

metric_name: Config 3: FC 73GB RAID-3x system MTBF per TB
notes: FC 73GB; RAID 3x; 51.37 RAID drives/TB; system MTBF = 2.665 years — MaXLine wins by 2.85x

metric_name: Config 4: MaXLine 250GB RAID-1x system MTBF per TB

metric_name: Config 4: FC 73GB RAID-1x system MTBF per TB

metric_name: Config 5: MaXLine 300GB RAID-2x vs FC 73GB RAID-2x
metric_value: MaXLine 13.69 years vs FC 3.997 years (Failure Ratio: 0.292)
notes: MaXLine 300GB; RAID 2x; 8.33 drives/TB vs FC 34.25 drives/TB; MaXLine wins by 3.42x

metric_name: Config 6: FC 146GB RAID-2x vs MaXLine 300GB RAID-2x
metric_value: MaXLine 13.69 years vs FC 7.995 years (Failure Ratio: 0.584)
notes: FC 146GB halves drive count vs 73GB but MaXLine still wins; 8.33 vs 17.12 drives/TB

metric_name: Config 7: FC 36.5GB RAID-5x vs MaXLine 300GB RAID-5x
metric_value: MaXLine 5.476 years vs FC 0.799 years (Failure Ratio: 0.146)
notes: Smallest FC drive (36.5GB); worst FC result; MaXLine wins by 6.85x — most extreme case

metric_name: Overall MTBF conclusion
metric_value: In all cases, MaXLine has superior system-level MTBF per TB
notes: Explicit conclusion statement in the source data

metric_name: Failure ratio range across all configs
metric_value: MaXLine failure ratio vs FC ranges from 0.146 to 0.584 — MaXLine always fails less per TB
notes: Consistent MaXLine win across all 7 configurations; ratio improves with smaller FC drives

metric_name: Drive count advantage mechanism
metric_value: MaXLine requires 3-7x fewer drives per TB than FC 73GB — this is the system MTBF advantage source
notes: 5 drives/TB (MaXLine 250GB) vs 17.12 drives/TB (FC 73GB) at RAID-1x; 3.42x drive count advantage

metric_name: System MTBF per TB as correct reliability metric
metric_value: System-level MTBF per TB (not individual drive MTBF) is the appropriate metric for storage purchasing decisions
notes: If adopted as standard metric; midline ATA drives are demonstrably more reliable per TB than FC
